native_ppt_primitives: native editable PPT text boxes for before and after labels
native editable PPT rectangle, line, and connector groups for mini slide thumbnails
native editable PPT emphasis object for the after-state proof object
components.layout_contract: Reserve the main canvas for two thumbnail states: the before thumbnail uses equal panels, and the after thumbnail enlarges one supported object with subordinate labels.
components.density_contract: Use one claim, two thumbnail panels, one dominant object, and fewer than 48 visible words; route extra evidence to trace.
components.generator_prompt: Draw the before/after transformation as original native editable PPT shapes and labels; do not paste a reference or screenshot.
components.qa_probe: From the contact sheet, the reviewer can identify before/after direction, the enlarged object, and the hierarchy change.
failure_modes: abstract process boxes with no thumbnail

native_ppt_primitives: native editable PPT mini slide frame
native editable PPT title, claim, metric, and gate labels
native editable PPT grouped object showing selected memory and density controls
components.layout_contract: Place a clear mini-preview object on the canvas, not a generic workflow card; it should read like a generated slide target with visible control zones.
components.density_contract: Use one mini-preview object, no more than two panels, no more than three control labels, and fewer than 52 visible words; no explanatory paragraph inside the preview.
components.generator_prompt: Build an original mini-preview from native editable PPT frames, labels, and control marks that show how the product chooses a slide output.
components.qa_probe: The contact sheet reads as a slide mini-preview, not a pasted screenshot or a generic dashboard.

native_ppt_primitives: native editable PPT rhythm strip labels
native editable PPT beat markers and density bars
native editable PPT focal object scale markers
components.layout_contract: Use the canvas to show rhythm variance: orientation beats stay sparse, proof beats add detail after the focal object, and climax increases object scale.
components.density_contract: Expose visible words, panels, and proof object count in trace; keep orientation slides sparse and avoid hiding density with smaller type.
components.generator_prompt: Convert audio pacing observations into a native editable rhythm strip and panel budget that changes slide density across the sequence.
components.qa_probe: The contact sheet shows rhythm difference between sparse orientation, proof, and climax slides.
failure_modes: all slides share the same density
dense strip competes with the focal object

native_ppt_primitives: native editable PPT route marker for trace-only detail
components.layout_contract: Use the canvas for one compressed headline object and one support line; transcript-like detail is represented by a small route marker outside the main claim area.
components.density_contract: Use one headline, one support line, one route marker, panels capped at one, and fewer than 38 visible words; never paste transcript paragraphs.
components.generator_prompt: Compress long spoken or written explanation into native editable headline structure and record the transcript anchor only in trace metadata.
components.qa_probe: The contact sheet shows a readable headline instead of transcript-like body text.
failure_modes: route marker becomes a status grid

native_ppt_primitives: native editable PPT dominant outcome object
native editable PPT public-blocked release gate
components.layout_contract: Reserve at least 45 percent of the climax canvas for one dominant outcome object; the close should use a decision handoff gate instead of an appendix-like grid.
components.density_contract: Use one dominant object, no more than three supporting labels, no more than three panels, and fewer than 50 visible words.
components.generator_prompt: Build the public-demo climax as a native editable outcome object with clear release gate support; do not repeat the process grid.
components.qa_probe: The contact sheet shows a visually distinct climax object and a close that reads as a decision handoff.
failure_modes: outcome object is smaller than proof cards

qa_gates: each component references a known visual learning target
each component requires native editable PPT primitives
component contracts contain no copied media references
public release remains blocked until render, provenance, and human approval
